concepts.title: Preparation and Properties of Sodium peroxide
concepts.conceptBody:
Sodium peroxide $\left( \mathbf{N}{{\mathbf{a}}_{\mathbf{2}}}{{\mathbf{O}}_{\mathbf{2}}} \right)$ 

It is a pale yellow powder. It is used as an oxidizing agent

$\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{ice-coldwater}2\text{NaOH}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

$\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{25{}^\circ C}2\text{NaOH}+{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

$\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

concepts.title: Preparation and Properties of Caustic soda
Preparation and Properties of Sodium carbonate or Washing soda
Preparation and Properties of Sodium bicarbonate or Baking soda
concepts.conceptBody:
Sodium Bicarbonate (Baking soda) 

It is used as a medicine to neutralize the acidity in stomach. It is also used in fire extinguishers.

$2\text{NaHC}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\xrightarrow{\Delta }\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}+\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

concepts.title: Sodium ThioSulphate
concepts.conceptBody:
Sodium thiosulphate, $\mathbf{N}{{\mathbf{a}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{~}{{\mathbf{S}}_{\mathbf{2}}}{{\mathbf{O}}_{\mathbf{3}}}\mathbf{\times 5}{{\mathbf{H}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{O}$ 

It is also know as Hypo.

Action on copper sulphate: Cuprous thio sulphate is formed which dissolves in excess of sodium thio sulphate to form a complex.

$\text{CuS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{Cu}{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$

$2\text{Cu}{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{C}{{\text{u}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{6}}$

$3\text{Cu}{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+2\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{4}}\left[ \text{C}{{\text{u}}_{6}}{{\left( ~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}} \right)}_{5}} \right]$

                                  $\text{ Sodium cuprothiosulphate }$

Some more compounds of sodium

1. Glauber's salt, $\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}\cdot 10{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

2. Cryolite, $\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{3}}\text{Al}{{\text{F}}_{6}}$

3. Soda Felspar, $\text{NaAlS}{{\text{i}}_{3}}{{\text{O}}_{8}}$

concepts.title: Other compounds of Potassium
concepts.conceptBody:
2. Potassium carbonate, ${{\mathbf{K}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{C}{{\mathbf{O}}_{\mathbf{3}}}$ 
It is also called potash or pearl ash.
${{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{{}}2\text{KOH}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$
3. Potassium sulphate 
${{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+\text{A}{{\text{l}}_{2}}{{\left( \text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}} \right)}_{3}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}\cdot \text{A}{{\text{l}}_{2}}{{\left( \text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}} \right)}_{3}}\cdot 24{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$
                                                       (potash alum)
${{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+4\text{C}\xrightarrow{\Delta }{{\text{K}}_{2}}~\text{S}+4\text{CO}(\uparrow )$
4. Potassium iodide, KI 
$\text{AgN}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+\text{KI}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{AgI}+\text{KN}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$ 
$\text{Pb}{{\left( \text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\text{COO} \right)}_{2}}+2\text{KI}\xrightarrow{{}}$ $\underset{\text{(yellow)}}{\mathop{\text{Pb}{{\text{I}}_{2}}}}\,+2\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{COO}$
$\text{HgC}{{\text{l}}_{2}}+2\text{KI}\xrightarrow{{}}\underset{\text{ (red ppt}\text{.) }\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,}{\mathop{\text{Hg}{{\text{I}}_{2}}+2\text{KCl}}}\,$
$\text{Hg}{{\text{I}}_{2}}+\underset{(excess)}{\mathop{2\text{KI}}}\,\xrightarrow{{}}\underset{(nessler's\,\,reagent)}{\mathop{{{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{Hg}{{\text{I}}_{4}}}}\,$
